whatThisIs: Search the CMS ICD-10-PCS code set - the seven-character procedure codes used on inpatient hospital claims. Unlike CPT, each PCS character is a position with its own meaning (section, body system, root operation, body part, approach, device, qualifier), so a code like 0DTJ4ZZ decomposes into a specific surgical action rather than a flat description. Sophie Well lets you type a fragment of the description ("appendectomy", "laparoscopic") or a partial code and filters the table in place.
whenToUse: Use this when a coder needs to confirm the correct PCS code for an inpatient procedure, when an audit query challenges a coded procedure and you need to look up the official tabular description, or when you are translating an operative note's plain-English action into the seven-character grammar PCS expects. ICD-10-PCS drives MS-DRG assignment for inpatient stays, so a wrong character (especially in the root-operation or approach positions) can shift the entire DRG and payment - reference lookup is faster and safer than guessing.
